summary: An overview of Martin Heidegger's philosophical project in Being and Time, focusing on his attempt to uncover the meaning of being through an existential analysis of Dasein. It details his critique of traditional substance ontology and introduces his concepts of being-in-the-world, readiness-to-hand (availableness), and the relational totality of equipment.

principles.statement: The essence of Dasein lies in its existence.
principles.rationale: Unlike other entities that possess fixed, determinative properties, human beings are entities that interpret their own essence. Our essence is open, never fixed once and for all, because our own being is an issue for us.
principles.source_quote: the ‘essence’ of Dasein lies in its existence

principles.statement: The question of being has priority over all other scientific inquiries.
principles.rationale: Every positive science operates within a pre-theoretical understanding of its subject matter. Without an explicitly developed ontology, sciences risk being led astray by unfounded metaphysical assumptions.
principles.source_quote: The question of being, Heidegger argues, has priority over all other scientific inquiries because every science presupposes a certain ontological understanding of its subject matter.

principles.rationale: Dasein and the world are fundamentally misunderstood if taken as two self-sufficient entities that subsequently enter into an external relationship. We are always already embodied and dealing with a meaningful world.
principles.source_quote: unitary phenomenon" that can only be understood when "seen as a whole

frameworks.name: Hermeneutic Phenomenology
frameworks.when_to_apply: When seeking to uncover the essential structures of Dasein and the meaning of being.
steps: Start with Dasein as it shows up in our pre-reflective, everyday understanding.
Offer a description that exhibits the essential structures determinative of Dasein's being.
Repeat the preparatory analytic on a higher, authentically ontological basis to uncover the sense of being (temporality).
frameworks.source_quote: description that exhibits essential structures... interpreting or laying out what we already tacitly understand

mental_models.name: Availableness (Readiness-to-hand) vs. Occurrentness (Presence-at-hand)
mental_models.description: A lens for distinguishing how we encounter entities. We primarily interact with things practically as useful equipment integrated into our projects (available). We only encounter them as mere physical objects with measurable properties (occurrent) when we step back to theorize or when the equipment breaks.
mental_models.example: A pen is usually transparent to us as we write (available). If it breaks or is too heavy, its physical properties become conspicuous, or if we study it scientifically, it becomes occurrent.

mental_models.name: Equipment Totality
mental_models.description: The understanding that no object exists in isolation; an entity is defined structurally as a node in a network of relations to other tools, human activities, and purposes.
mental_models.example: A pen fills a place defined by its relationships to paper, ink, desks, and the human activity of writing or paying bills.

heuristics: To understand the meaning of being, look first to the entity for whom being is an issue (Dasein).
Analyze entities in the context of their everyday use before analyzing their theoretical or physical properties.

signature_quotes.text: work out concretely the question concerning the sense of ‘being’
signature_quotes.context: Describing the primary aim of Being and Time.

signature_quotes.text: the ‘essence’ of Dasein lies in its existence
signature_quotes.context: Explaining that human beings do not have fixed properties like objects, but rather interpret and take a stand on their own being.

signature_quotes.text: Dasein is an entity which is in each case I myself
signature_quotes.context: Highlighting the 'mineness' of Dasein and personal responsibility for existence.
Defining how we encounter everyday objects relationally rather than as isolated substances.

anti_patterns.description: Treating human beings as substances with fixed essential properties (like rationality or consciousness).
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It assumes human beings are ontologically homogenous with other entities, ignoring that Dasein's essence is open and defined by its existence and self-interpretation.

anti_patterns.description: Understanding 'being-in-the-world' on the model of spatial containment.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It imports occurrentist assumptions, treating Dasein and the world as two physical objects inside one another, rather than recognizing 'being-in' as being familiar with or at home in a meaningful environment.

anti_patterns.description: Viewing theoretical or scientific understanding as the primary or most fundamental way to know the world.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Theoretical understanding is actually a derivative mode that abstracts away from our primary, everyday practical concern and the significance things usually have for us.

anti_patterns.description: Dismissing the question of being because it is supposedly self-evident or undefinable.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: The mere appeal to what is well understood, without further illumination, conceals a superficial and mistaken grasp of the matter.
